findings.message: Breaking schema change: field 'name' renamed to 'title' without a compatible migration path. Existing GraphQL clients querying 'Item.name' will break.
findings.suggestion: To maintain backwards compatibility, keep both 'name' and 'title' fields temporarily, with 'name' being a deprecated alias, OR provide a schema versioning/pagination strategy that allows clients to opt into the new API version.

findings.message: Inconsistency: mutation 'create_item' parameter is named 'name' but the returned Item type now uses 'title'. This creates a mismatch between input and output schema.
findings.suggestion: Update the mutation parameter to accept 'title' instead of 'name', or provide both for transition. Ensure input/output field names are consistent and semantically aligned.

findings.message: The 'description' field is typed as optional but always returns None. This suggests incomplete implementation or missing data population from the ItemModel.
findings.suggestion: Either populate 'description' from ItemModel (if the field exists there), or clarify the intent. If description is not yet implemented, consider using a separate query or adding the field to ItemModel and _to_item mapping.
